text:
Project Title:______________
VitaFAST
Brief, descriptive name for the therapeutic program
Therapeutic Concept Summary:______________
One-paragraph overview of the approach and its novelty
Discovery and development of small-molecule activators of autophagy that rescue lysosomal dysfunction and cellular homeostasis. <Scientific_claim>Compounds are identified via high-throughput phenotypic screening in autophagy-deficient cell models and optimized as first-in-class autophagy activators for aging and lysosomal storage disorders.
</Scientific_claim>Target Indication:______________
Specific disease or condition being addressed
Longevity, Niemann-Pick type C (NPC), and other lysosomal storage disorders
Patient Population Size:______________
Estimated number of patients who could benefit from the therapy
The VitaFAST program addresses multiple patient populations across its target indications:
claims: Compounds are identified via high-throughput phenotypic screening in autophagy-deficient cell models and optimized as first-in-class autophagy activators for aging and lysosomal storage disorders.

text:
- Niemann-Pick type C (NPC): Ultra-rare genetic disorder with an estimated prevalence of ~1 per 100,000-120,000 live births globally. Diagnosed prevalent cases estimated at ~787 patients in the 7 major markets (2022), with ~335 in the US alone. Total NPC therapeutics market valued at ~$110-140 million (2024), projected to reach ~$210-260 million by 2032.
- Lysosomal storage disorders (broader category): Estimated frequency of ~1 in 5,000 live births collectively. Global LSD therapeutics market valued at ~$9-11 billion (2025), growing at 4.5-8.4% CAGR to reach ~$13-20 billion by 2032-2034.
- Longevity/aging population: Universal applicability - the entire aging population experiences autophagy decline. Global longevity and anti-aging drugs market estimated at $8.9 billion (2023), projected to reach $24.5 billion by 2032 (11.8% CAGR).
- Broader neurodegeneration: Alzheimer's disease (50+ million patients globally), Parkinson's disease (10+ million globally) - conditions where autophagy dysfunction plays a key pathological role.
Mechanism of Action:______________

Detailed explanation of how the intervention produces therapeutic effect (if considered non-confidential)
Small molecules that activate autophagy and restore lysosomal function, rescuing cell survival in models with lysosomal dysfunction (Npc1-/-) but not in cells lacking autophagy initiation (Atg5-/-).
Biological Target:______________
Specific molecular target (protein, pathway, gene) being modulated(if considered non-confidential)

Current Development Stage:______________
Clear statement of experimental progress achieved to date (e.g., computational modeling only, in vitro validation, in vivo studies, candidate selection)
Testing new chemical entities in vitro, also exploring the feasibility of a drug repurposing clinical trial
Next 3 major milestones (with estimated dates)

<Scientific_claim>- Pilot screening of FDA-approved drug library identified 12 previously unknown autophagy inducers ✓
- Thousands of compounds from natural and commercial libraries screened ✓
- Multiple hit series with autophagy-activating signatures identified ✓
- Three NCE rounds ordered and tested (Q4 2024 - H2 2025) ✓
- Three lead compounds identified with 10-100x higher potency than previous series ✓
- Two novel targets identified (H1 2025) - previously proposed longevity targets ✓
- Commercially available compounds binding these targets validated as potent autophagy inducers ✓</Scientific_claim>

text:
- Q4 2025: Complete computational optimization with known targets, IP filing for lead molecules
- Q4 2025 - Q1 2026: Drug repurposing trial scoping (UAE pathway) - regulatory pre-submission, CMC planning, clinical protocol draft
- 2026: IND-enabling studies (murine safety experiments) for novel lead compounds
- 2026-2027: Potential Phase 2 clinical trial initiation (repurposed compound pathway)

Team Expertise Areas:______________
Relevant backgrounds and capabilities of the team
Autophagy and lysosomal biology, cellular homeostasis and neurodegeneration, high-throughput screening and assay development, organic and medicinal chemistry, SAR and drug discovery, computational chemistry, and translational longevity therapeutics.

Whether patents are provisional, filed, or granted
IP strategy under development with fresh patent filings planned:
- IP-NFT registered on Molecule protocol, tokenized as VITA-FAST IP Tokens (first DeSci fractionalized IPT)
- Newcastle University collaboration IP subject to university IP framework
- New patent applications planned for novel compounds developed through SAR optimization (targeting H2 2025)
- Application IP filed 2nd Dec 2025 for repurposed compounds for autophagy induction and longevity use - Patent title - METHODS FOR ENHANCING AUTOPHAGY USING REPURPOSED PHARMACEUTICAL COMPOUNDS
- IP structure: VitaFast LLC (Delaware) formed to hold and manage project IP
Type of claims being pursued (composition of matter, method of use, target)

- Repurposing claims for commercially available compounds as autophagy activators
- Methods of use for treating lysosomal storage disorders via autophagy activation
- Methods of use for longevity and age-related disease treatment

- Composition of matter for novel autophagy-activating small molecules
- Methods of use for treating lysosomal storage disorders via autophagy activation
- Methods of use for longevity and age-related disease treatment
- Novel target applications for autophagy induction
- Screening assays and methods for identifying autophagy modulators
Known blocking patents or FTO concerns

section_heading: Favorable FTO position with key considerations:
text:
- VitaFAST's approach targets downstream autophagy activation and lysosomal function rescue, distinct from mTOR inhibitors (rapamycin and rapalogs)
- Novel compound structures are proprietary and distinct from existing autophagy modulators
- Phenotypic screening approach generates novel chemical matter
- Key competitors (rapamycin, everolimus) work via mTOR inhibition - VitaFAST compounds work via distinct, newly identified targets
- Two newly identified targets represent novel mechanism of action for autophagy activation
- Strong differentiation from existing therapeutic approaches for NPC (miglustat, cyclodextrins)

Proof-of-Concept Data:______________
<Scientific_claim>Experimental results supporting the mechanism with appropriate controls
- Autophagy-deficient cells (Npc1-/-, Atg5-/-, Atg7-/-, FIP200/Rb1cc1-/-) show conserved cell-death phenotype under galactose stress; rescue is achievable by autophagy induction or downstream interventions (e.g., NAD+ modulation), establishing the assay biology.
- Pilot screen of FDA-approved drugs identified 12 previously unknown autophagy inducers that activate autophagy flux in luciferase-p62 and LC3 'traffic-light' assays, validating the screening platform.
- Thousands of compounds from natural and commercial libraries have been screened; multiple hit series with autophagy-activating signatures have been identified and advanced to derivative synthesis.
</Scientific_claim>Disease Model Information:______________
Specific models used and their validation status
- Mouse embryonic fibroblasts (MEFs) with Npc1 knockout (Npc1-/-) modelling lysosomal dysfunction associated with NPC and Parkinson's disease.
claims:
Experimental results supporting the mechanism with appropriate controls
- Autophagy-deficient cells (Npc1-/-, Atg5-/-, Atg7-/-, FIP200/Rb1cc1-/-) show conserved cell-death phenotype under galactose stress; rescue is achievable by autophagy induction or downstream interventions (e.g., NAD+ modulation), establishing the assay biology.
- Pilot screen of FDA-approved drugs identified 12 previously unknown autophagy inducers that activate autophagy flux in luciferase-p62 and LC3 'traffic-light' assays, validating the screening platform.
- Thousands of compounds from natural and commercial libraries have been screened; multiple hit series with autophagy-activating signatures have been identified and advanced to derivative synthesis.

text:
- Autophagy-initiation knockouts (Atg5-/-, Atg7-/-, FIP200/Rb1cc1-/-) as negative controls to distinguish true autophagy-dependent rescue from non-specific survival effects.
Dose-Response Relationships: ______________
Quantitative potency data with IC50/EC50/ED50 values
Quantitative dose-response characterization ongoing. <Scientific_claim>Key findings to date:
- Third-round NCEs show 10-100x higher potency than earlier lead series
- Commercially available compounds binding newly identified targets show highest potency in autophagy assays
- Formal EC50/IC50 determination planned as part of lead optimization
- Potency benchmarking against rapamycin and other commercial autophagy inducers completed
Development Progress
Candidate Status:______________
</Scientific_claim>Number of candidates identified and optimization progress
Three lead structures from which variations on each are producing favourable New Chemical Entites.
Two lead repurposing molecules that have been through Phase 1 trials and are highly potent in lab assays of autophagy.

In vitro safety screening (TRL 2) or GLP toxicology status (TRL 3)
claims:
Key findings to date:

- Formal EC50/IC50 determination planned as part of lead optimization

Development Progress
Candidate Status:______________

text:
- Lead compounds tested in cellular autophagy assays with no acute cytotoxicity signals at therapeutic concentrations
- Rescue is specific to autophagy-competent cells (Npc1-/-) and not observed in autophagy-deficient cells (Atg5-/-), demonstrating mechanism specificity
- Worm healthspan and longevity studies ongoing to assess systemic effects

- Murine safety experiments as IND-enabling activities
- Standard ADMET profiling for lead candidates
- GLP toxicology package for clinical development

Manufacturing Route:______________
Synthesis approach and scale-up demonstration

- Synthesis performed by contracted chemistry suppliers
- SAR-driven optimization yielding drug-like molecules
- Scale-up synthesis planned following lead selection
- Keele University (Dr. Jóhannes Reynisson) providing medicinal chemistry and SAR guidance
- Novosibirsk Institute of Organic Chemistry (Prof. Konstantin Volcho) contributing natural product library synthesis

- GMP manufacturing scoping initiated (VFDP-14)
- Manufacturing timeline and cost identified as key risk factors
- Existing GMP process for the repurposed compound will be utilised
Regulatory Pathway:______________
Identified FDA pathway and clinical endpoint strategy
Clinical trial pathway in UAE. Blood autophagy/mitophagy biomarkers testing.

Market Size:______________
Peak sales potential or total addressable market estimate
Primary indication - Lysosomal storage disorders:
- Global LSD therapeutics market: ~$9-11 billion (2025), projected to reach ~$13-20 billion by 2032-2034
- Enzyme replacement therapy (ERT) dominates market (~84% share in 2024)
- Niemann-Pick type C market: ~$110-140 million (2024), projected to reach ~$210-260 million by 2032
- Strong unmet need for therapies that restore lysosomal function at the cellular level

- Global longevity and anti-aging drugs market: $8.9 billion (2023) → $24.5 billion by 2032 (11.8% CAGR)
- mTOR inhibitors represent key segment within longevity market
- Autophagy inducers represent emerging subsegment with significant growth potential

- Alzheimer's disease therapeutics: ~$10 billion (2024), projected to grow significantly with disease-modifying therapies
- Parkinson's disease therapeutics: ~$5 billion market
- Autophagy dysfunction implicated in both conditions
Competitive Position:______________
Key competitors and differentiation strategy

- Rapamycin/Rapalogs (sirolimus, everolimus, temsirolimus): FDA-approved mTOR inhibitors; well-established mechanism but significant side effects (immunosuppression); work by inhibiting mTOR upstream of autophagy
- Miglustat (Zavesca/Brazaves): Approved for NPC in EU/Japan (not US); substrate reduction therapy; limited efficacy in clinical use
- Cyclodextrins (Trappsol Cyclo, Kleptose HPB): Compassionate use for NPC; target cholesterol clearance rather than autophagy directly
- Metformin: AMPK activator; autophagy-inducing effects secondary to metabolic mechanism

<Scientific_claim>- Novel mechanism: Direct autophagy activation via newly identified targets (distinct from mTOR inhibition)
- Lysosomal-specific rescue: Compounds rescue lysosomal dysfunction specifically, not just autophagy initiation
- High potency: Lead compounds show 10-100x higher potency than earlier series
- NAD preservation: Mechanism directly addresses autophagy-NAD axis identified by Korolchuk lab
●
- Dual development paths: Novel NCEs and drug repurposing opportunities
●
- First-in-class potential: No approved drugs specifically targeting autophagy activation for longevity</Scientific_claim>
claims:
- Novel mechanism: Direct autophagy activation via newly identified targets (distinct from mTOR inhibition)
- Lysosomal-specific rescue: Compounds rescue lysosomal dysfunction specifically, not just autophagy initiation
- High potency: Lead compounds show 10-100x higher potency than earlier series
- NAD preservation: Mechanism directly addresses autophagy-NAD axis identified by Korolchuk lab
●
- Dual development paths: Novel NCEs and drug repurposing opportunities
●

section_heading: Key foundational publications from Korolchuk Lab:
text:
- Kataura T et al. (2024) "Targeting the autophagy-NAD axis protects against cell death in Niemann-Pick type C1 disease models" - Cell Death & Disease. <Scientific_claim>Demonstrates NAD preservation via autophagy in NPC disease models.
- Kataura T et al. (2022)</Scientific_claim> "Autophagy promotes cell survival by maintaining NAD levels" Developmental Cell. Establishes evolutionarily conserved role of autophagy in NAD preservation.
- Wilson N et al. (2023) "The autophagy-NAD axis in longevity and disease" - Trends in Cell Biology. Review of autophagy-NAD crosstalk and therapeutic implications.
- Sedlackova L et al. (2023) "Metabolic function of autophagy is essential for cell survival" - Autophagy. Commentary on autophagy-NAD connection and therapeutic targets.
- Sedlackova L & Korolchuk VI (2020) "The crosstalk of NAD, ROS and autophagy in cellular health and ageing" - Biogerontology. Review of NAD-ROS-autophagy interplay in aging.
claims:
Demonstrates NAD preservation via autophagy in NPC disease models.
- Kataura T et al. (2022)

section_heading: Key foundational publications from Korolchuk Lab:
text: - Sun C et al. (2023) "NAD depletion mediates cytotoxicity in human neurons with autophagy deficiency" - Cell Reports. Human neuronal validation of autophagy-NAD axis.

- Autophagy dysfunction is well-established in NPC, neurodegeneration, and aging
- NAD depletion in autophagy deficiency validated across yeast, mouse, and human models
- Pharmacological and genetic interventions targeting this cascade improve survival in autophagy-deficient cells

- P62 clearance assays - primary autophagy flux measurement
- LC3 "traffic-light" assays - autophagy activation confirmation
- Lysosomal function markers (lysosomal fusion assessment)
- Blood autophagy/mitophagy biomarkers (planned for clinical pathway in UAE)
- NAD levels - metabolic rescue confirmation

- Genetic testing to confirm NPC1/NPC2 mutations
- Baseline lysosomal function assessment
- Disease severity staging
Longevity/aging biomarkers (for clinical trial):
- Autophagy markers in peripheral blood cells
- NAD+ levels in blood
- Mitochondrial function markers
- Inflammatory markers (as autophagy restores homeostasis)

section_heading: Platform Potential:______________ Additional indications or broader technology applications
text:
The VitaFAST autophagy activation platform has broad applicability:
- Neurodegeneration: Alzheimer's disease (protein aggregate clearance), Parkinson's disease (mitochondrial quality control), Huntington's disease
- Other lysosomal storage disorders: Beyond NPC - Gaucher disease, Fabry disease, mucopolysaccharidoses
- Metabolic diseases: Conditions where autophagy decline contributes to pathology
- Aging/longevity: Systemic cellular health maintenance
- Cancer: Context-dependent application (autophagy-dependent cell death in some cancers)
- Combination approaches: Potential synergy with NAD+ precursors (NMN, NR), sirtuin activators, or other geroprotectors

- Ongoing governance-approved budgets: VFDP-11 (Q4 2024), VFDP-12, VFDP-13 ($135K H1 2025), VFDP-14 ($30K repurposing scoping), VFDP-15 (modified H2 2025 plan)
